Off Calendar May-19-2017 Continued To Jul-12-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-16-2017 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ANGELINA MOYO OF MOYO LAW, TEL. NO. 1-916-667-3262 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. PATRICK M. CALLAHAN OF BURNHAM/BROWN, TEL. NO. 1-510-835-6802 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendants Walgreen Co. and Andrew MacDonald's demurrer to both causes of action in the first amended complaint is overruled as to both causes of action. Liberally construed, the first amended complaint (especially paragraph 27 therein) adequately alleges that defendants knowingly made a false statement that plaintiff Mara Mendoza falsified her time card to at least one prospective employer of Ms. Mendoza. This allegation is sufficient to allege malice to preclude the privilege defense codified at Civil Code 47(c). While the better course would have been for Ms. Mendoza to seek leave to allege a Labor Code 1050 claim, there would have been no basis to deny her leave to do so and thus there is no basis to sustain a demurrer on that ground. The single publication rule is a rule about the accrual of the statute of limitations and does not preclude a party from asserting multiple theories of liability based on the same publication. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =302/hk

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-16-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ANGELINA MOYO OF MOYO LAW, TEL. NO. 1-916-667-3262 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. PATRICK M. CALLAHAN OF BURNHAM/BROWN, TEL. NO. 1-510-835-6802 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendants Walgreen Co. and Andrew MacDonald's motion to strike portions of the first amended complaint is granted without leave to amend as to the punitive damages allegations against Walgreen and denied as to all other allegations sought to be stricken. The first amended complaint does not allege sufficient facts showing ratification or advance knowledge of Walgreen. Because Ms. Mendoza was given the opportunity to adequately allege punitive damages liability against Walgreen in the order filed January 11, 2017 and she has not done so, it does not appear reasonably possible that she is able to do so. Liberally construed, the first amended complaint alleges despicable conduct by Mr. MacDonald. While the better course would have been for Ms. Mendoza to seek leave to allege a Labor Code 1050 claim, there would have been no basis to deny her leave to do so and thus there is no basis to grant a motion to strike on that ground. The single publication rule is a rule about the accrual of the statute of limitations and does not preclude a party from asserting multiple theories of liability based on the same publication.
Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =302/hk

Off Calendar Jan-10-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ANGELINA MOYO OF THE BOWMAN & ASSOCIATES, TEL. NO. 1-916-985-2600 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. SHAUDEE NAVID OF THE BURNHAM & BROWN LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-510-835-6823 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The court modified its tentative ruling and ruled as follows: Defendants Walgreen Co. and Andrew MacDonald's motion to strike the punitive damages allegations in the complaint filed by plaintiff Mara Mendoza is granted as to both Wallgreens and Mr. McDonald with 20 days leave to amend as to both if the plaintiff can in good faith allege a basis for punitive damage against one or both. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/hk
